description: Ground-truth fixtures for kb search precision@1 and recall@5 evaluation. Each fixture has a query, the issue/alert text it derives from, and the wiki/snapshot pages that should appear in results.

fixtures.query: set up scheduled cron job to send daily report
fixtures.issueText: We need an internal agent to email a daily activity report at 9am UTC. What's the OpenClaw-native way to schedule this?
fixtures.rationale: The user wants exact-time scheduling. cron-jobs.md is the canonical answer; automation/index.md is the decision guide that points there.

fixtures.issueText: I want to build a flow that ingests an issue, searches the wiki, generates a work packet, and saves it as an artifact. Each step needs to be durable so we can retry on failure.
fixtures.rationale: Rivet Workflows is the most token-relevant result for 'durable multi-step workflow with branching' in the expanded corpus. The OpenClaw Task Flow page (taskflow.md) does not appear in top 5 — its filename doesn't match query tokens, so path-weight scoring places it below rivet-workflows. This may indicate a ranking bias worth addressing (see KB issue: BM-AS/agent-architect/issues).

fixtures.query: run a second isolated openclaw gateway in docker on different port
fixtures.issueText: I want to run a POC OpenClaw gateway alongside the existing one on this machine. It must use a non-default host port and not collide with the running gateway.
fixtures.rationale: Three pages cover this: docker install, multi-gateway pattern, and gateway config for ports/dirs.

fixtures.query: create a SKILL.md file with description and instructions
fixtures.issueText: How do I add a file-based skill to my agent so it can use a specific procedure when triggered?
fixtures.rationale: Both standing-orders.md and skills.md have equal token hit counts (11). The shorter document (standing-orders.md, 10KB) ranks above skills.md (16KB) due to BM25 length normalization. This is a ranking artifact, not semantic relevance — skills.md is the correct answer for 'how to add a file-based skill'. See KB issue: BM-AS/agent-architect/issues for ranking algorithm review.

fixtures.query: track long running background work as task ledger
fixtures.issueText: I have a sub-agent doing a long research run. I need to see its status, audit when it ran, and pick it up later.
fixtures.rationale: Background tasks ledger is the OpenClaw primitive for tracking detached work; index.md is the decision guide.
